response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished polyglot web and full-stack engineer with some systems-level background (C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services, Linux), but he does not meet the core requirements of this role. There is no native-level Japanese proficiency (his languages are English, Romanian, and basic German), which is a hard requirement. His career centers on web, mobile, and cloud product work rather than embedded firmware; there is no evidence of Linux kernel investigation or modification, LAN-switch or L2/L3 networking development, or security technologies such as MACsec, 802.1X, or encryption/key-exchange implementations.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with systems exposure: C/C++ IDE components, low-latency asynchronous services, and Linux experience
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and sustained open-source contributions
Demonstrated ability to learn new stacks quickly and own products end-to-end across domains
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency, a stated hard requirement for the role
No embedded networking, switch firmware, or Linux kernel source-modification experience
No demonstrated security-domain work (MACsec, 802.1X, authentication systems, tamper detection, or encryption)

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or Linux kernel experience
General systems programming and C/C++ background
